name: MS-QRD-010: Ransomware Network Activity — SMB Propagation and C2 Staging
description: Detects network-level indicators of active ransomware attacks by correlating three concurrent behavioral patterns: (1) high-volume internal SMB traffic consistent with network share enumeration and file encryption across shared drives, (2) simultaneous outbound connections to external IPs on ports associated with ransomware C2 and data staging, and (3) spikes in DNS queries that may indicate domain generation algorithm (DGA) activity used by modern ransomware families for C2 resilience. Targets modern ransomware families including LockBit, Conti, BlackCat/ALPHV, Cl0p, and Play which combine encryption with double-extortion exfiltration.
author: MilliSec Blue Team — Rihad Ebilov

qradar.aql: SELECT sourceip AS "Source IP (Ransomware Host)", LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS "Log Source", COUNT(*) AS "Total Events", COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) AS "Unique Destinations", SUM(CASE WHEN destinationport IN (445, 139) AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Internal SMB Events", SUM(CASE WHEN NOT (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) AND destinationport IN (80, 443, 8080, 8443, 9001) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "External C2 Events", SUM(CASE WHEN destinationport = 53 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "DNS Query Events", MIN(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "First Seen", MAX(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "Last Seen", CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN destinationport IN (445, 139) AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 10 AND SUM(CASE WHEN NOT (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) AND destinationport IN (80, 443, 8080, 8443, 9001) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 1 THEN 'CRITICAL — Active Ransomware: SMB Spread + C2 Confirmed' WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN destinationport IN (445, 139) AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 10 THEN 'HIGH — Mass SMB Activity: Ransomware Propagation Suspected' WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN destinationport = 53 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 50 AND SUM(CASE WHEN NOT (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) AND destinationport IN (80, 443, 8080, 8443, 9001) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 1 THEN 'HIGH — DGA + External C2: Pre-Encryption Stage Suspected' ELSE 'MEDIUM — Elevated Activity: Investigate' END AS "Threat Assessment" FROM events WHERE sourceip IS NOT NULL AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', sourceip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', sourceip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', sourceip)) AND (destinationport IN (445, 139, 53, 80, 443, 8080, 8443, 9001) OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%ransomware%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%smb%') GROUP BY sourceip, LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) HAVING (SUM(CASE WHEN destinationport IN (445, 139) AND (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 10) OR (SUM(CASE WHEN destinationport = 53 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 50 AND SUM(CASE WHEN NOT (INCIDR('10.0.0.0/8', destinationip) OR INCIDR('172.16.0.0/12', destinationip) OR INCIDR('192.168.0.0/16', destinationip)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 1) ORDER BY "Internal SMB Events" DESC LAST 10 MINUTES
qradar.offense_relevance: This rule detects the network signature of an active ransomware attack by simultaneously tracking three kill-chain phases: (1) Propagation — mass SMB connections to internal hosts for share enumeration and cross-host encryption; (2) C2 staging — outbound connections confirming ransomware is communicating with attacker infrastructure; (3) DGA activity — high DNS query volume indicating domain generation algorithm used by ransomware for resilient C2. The 'Threat Assessment' CASE field instantly classifies attack stage. CRITICAL assessment (SMB spread + C2 confirmed) means encryption is actively in progress — every second counts. Correlate with Splunk MS-SPL-010 (file rename detection) for full kill-chain confirmation.
response_actions: CRITICAL: Pull network cable / disable switch port for source host IMMEDIATELY — do not wait for approval
Block all SMB (445/139) traffic between VLANs at core switch ACL
Disable all file shares on affected hosts via net share * /delete
Snapshot all VMs in the environment before ransomware reaches them
Contact backup team — verify last clean backup timestamp and integrity
Preserve network captures and memory dumps before isolation
Engage incident response team and notify management chain
Do NOT pay ransom without consulting legal and management
Document all affected systems for insurance and legal purposes
qradar.false_positive_notes: Legitimate file server backup jobs generating high SMB traffic. Domain controller SYSVOL replication. Mass file copy operations by authorized administrators. SCCM/GPO policy distribution generating SMB bursts. These are distinguishable from ransomware by: (1) absence of simultaneous external C2 connections, (2) source being a known server/DC rather than a workstation, (3) occurring during scheduled maintenance windows.
qradar.tuning_notes: The HAVING clause fires on either: (A) Internal SMB Events >= 10 in 10 minutes, OR (B) DNS >= 50 + any external connection. Condition A catches active propagation phase. Condition B catches pre-encryption DGA reconnaissance phase — earlier detection opportunity. Whitelist known file servers and DCs by adding AND sourceip NOT IN (SELECT value FROM REFERENCETABLE('DC_and_FileServer_IPs')) to WHERE clause. The 10-minute window is intentionally short — ransomware encrypts files extremely fast (LockBit: ~1000 files/min).
